response: The internet originated in the 1960s with ARPANET, which sent its first message in 1969 and adopted TCP/IP in 1983, laying the groundwork for today’s network. Tim Berners‑Lee’s 1989 invention of the World Wide Web (HTML, URLs, HTTP) and the 1993 release of the Mosaic browser made the web usable for the public, sparking the dot‑com boom of the late‑1990s and later the rise of social media, mobile internet after the 2007 iPhone launch, and cloud computing pioneered by AWS in 2006. Currently, over 5 billion people are online, and the internet underpins virtually all modern commerce, communication, and entertainment.
